texto: What did it matter that the Light had been taken away from him? What did it matter that Arienne was gone? If it took a thousand years to find her...if he had to cross boiling seas or raging skies...if it cost him his eyes or his tongue, his hands or his feet, his very soul... He would relinquish it all, if it meant he could keep that promise he'd made so long ago. He had nothing else. Without this, he knew, he would simply wither away.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de S.K. Michels que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “What did it matter that the Light had been taken away from him? What did it matter that Arienne w…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, light, promise, com foco em promise.

frase_original: What did it matter that the Light had been taken away from him? What did it matter that Arienne was gone? If it took a thousand years to find her...if he had to cross boiling seas or raging skies...if it cost him his eyes or his tongue, his hands or his feet, his very soul... He would relinquish it all, if it meant he could keep that promise he'd made so long ago. He had nothing else. Without this, he knew, he would simply wither away.

texto: The Light shone shone from her skin,casting about the room in dancing beams that warmed all those that gazed upon her. A magnificent creature,so powerful,so distant,so wonderous and strange and out of reach. It almost seemed she did not belong to this world,she should walk instead through palaces in the heavens,or labyrinths of deep seas. Forests where trees stretched out of sight,with canopies that spread for miles;or mountains that glittered white and violet.
explicacao: Orientação direta de S.K Michels sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “The Light shone shone from her skin,casting about the room in dancing beams that warmed all those…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, heavens, light, útil em momentos de light e magnificent.
